Binheap to follow within the day probably, but policies likely need a small update first, so will have to take a bit more time.

Patch Set 8:

I had to add some definitions that are used by the CRR-N/ORR/TRR policies that would otherwise not apply cleanly if they were placed in the policies' patches. Alternatively I can remove these and place them in the policies' patches if we could land the NRS framework and FIFO policy before the other policies, if that is easier.

Patch Set 13:

I realize I should have ideally posted these patches earlier during the feature landing window, but unfortunately I cannot undo things. Still, I am hoping there should be enough time to get these patches landed for the scheduled 2.4 release.

I know most people are very busy on a constant basis, but can I please ask if possible to try and carry out some inspections on these patches soon, ideally in the coming few days? I will produce updated patches as soon as I can in response to any comments in the hope of getting things ready on time.

Cheers

Patch Set 17: (1 inline comment)

Patch Set 18:

Just a quick question:

When a policy is enabled via lprocfs, only the regular NRS head policy instance is enabled by default i.e. the policy will only be used to handle regular priority requests, and high-priority requests will continue to be handled by the previously set policy (i.e. FIFO in the most common case). 

I did it this way because at the time I assumed it would be best for high priority requests to be handled as they come in from the network, without any processing in their ordering by default, unless the user specified otherwise. But I am not too sure about this anymore, I now think it may be best if both regular and hp requests are handled by default, unless otherwise specified.

This may only be a small detail, but if anyone has an opinion on this please let me know; I think I am inclined on making this change soon.

parents.subject: LU-2561 mdd: create decares attr set with parent attr

commit.subject: LU-398 ptlrpc: Add the NRS framework and FIFO policy
commit.message:
LU-398 ptlrpc: Add the NRS framework and FIFO policy

The Network Request Scheduler (NRS) allows a user to control the way
in which RPCs are dispatched from PTLRPC services. This can be used to
various effects, most importantly improved fairness across the
cluster, better I/O performance under certain workloads, and allowing
for QoS semantics to be used at the filesystem level. Central to the
operation of NRS are NRS policies, each of which implements handling
of RPCs in a different way, aiming to achieve a particular goal.

This patch adds the core NRS framework and the NRS FIFO policy, which
is a logical wrapper around previous, non-NRS functionality, and is
used as the default policy for handling all types of RPCs.

Signed-off-by: Nikitas Angelinas <nikitas_angelinas@xyratex.com>
Co-authored-by: Liang Zhen <liang@whamcloud.com>
Change-Id: I7fcd7885cc89b653d9bc482da533aae2f129bdf9
Oracle-bug-id: b=13634
Xyratex-bug-id: MRP-73
